lines.en_lit: And wasn’t it taught Yosef of Hutzal is the Yosef the Babylonian, he is Isi ben Yehuda, he is Isi ben Gur Arye, he is Isi ben Gamliel; he is Isi ben Mahalalel? And what is his name? Isi ben Akiva is his name. In the Torah no in the Prophets there are

And in the Torah are there no But there is dilemma that Rav Ḥisda raised “And he sent the young men of the children of Israel who sacrificed burnt-offerings” sheep, “And sacrificed peace-offerings of bulls to the Lord,” Or perhaps these and these bulls, according to Rav Ḥisda was uncertain obvious to Isi ben Yehuda.

MISHNA: The outer was hooked [perufa] on the south and inner hooked on the north walks between until he reaches the north. he reaches the north, turns his face to the south, walks to his left along the curtain until he reaches the Ark.

he reaches the Ark, he places the coal pan between the two staves. He piles the incense atop coals, and the whole chamber in its entirety would fill with smoke. He exits and comes the way he entered. And he recites a brief prayer in the outer chamber, And he would not extend his prayer so as not to alarm the Jewish people,
GEMARA: With what are we dealing If we say about the First Temple, were there curtains Rather, with the Second Temple, was there an Ark

But wasn’t it taught when Ark was buried, along with it was buried the jar of manna and the flask of oil used for anointing, and Aaron’s staff with its almonds and blossoms, and the chest that the Philistines sent as a gift [doron] to the God of Israel as it is stated: “And put the jewels of gold that you return to Him for a guilt-offering, in a coffer by its side, and send it away that it may go”
And who buried Josiah, buried it. And what did he see that bury it? He saw that it is written: “The Lord will bring you, and your king whom you shall set over you, he arose and buried it.

As it is stated: “And he said to the Levites who taught all of Israel, who were sacred to the Lord: Put the sacred Ark in the house that Solomon, son of David, king of Israel, built. There shall no more be a burden upon your shoulders; now serve the Lord your God, and His people Israel”
And Rabbi Elazar said: is derived there there, And it is derived “Generations” “generations”
Actually, to the Second Temple, and what is Reaches the Ark? place of the Ark. But it is taught he places the coal pan between the two staves, say: As between the two staves,

He piles the incense atop coals. We learned in accordance with the one who said he piles It was taught one He piles closest which is farthest from And it was taught in another He piles it farthest which is closest to him.
Abaye said this is tanna’im. And Abaye said: It is reasonable in accordance with the one who said closest which is farthest from him. As we learned they teach Be careful
